title: Setup Foundation and Configuration
summary: Initialize project foundation including Polish language configuration, playful fonts, colorful Tailwind theme, and installation of required dependencies like framer-motion and lucide-react.
acceptanceCriteria: app/layout.tsx has lang="pl" and uses a playful Google Font (e.g., Balsamiq Sans or Nunito).
tailwind.config.ts and app/globals.css are updated with bright, high-contrast colors.
framer-motion and lucide-react dependencies are installed and configured.

title: Database Schema and Server Actions
summary: Create the database schema using Drizzle ORM to track global interactions, and implement Next.js Server Actions to fetch and update these stats.
acceptanceCriteria: lib/db/schema.ts includes an `interactions` table utilizing the `DATABASE_TABLE_PREFIX` environment variable.

title: Core Interactive Components (Hero and Objects)
summary: Develop the animated Reksio hero component and reusable interactive background objects using Framer Motion.
acceptanceCriteria: components/HeroReksio.tsx is created with hover and tap animations using framer-motion.
components/InteractiveObject.tsx is created as a reusable animated component.
All interactive elements use large touch targets (min 48x48px) suitable for children on touch devices.

title: Mini-Game Implementation
summary: Build the "Znajdź kości" (Find the bones) interactive mini-game and integrate it with the server actions to track global score.
acceptanceCriteria: components/MiniGame.tsx is implemented with local React state to track found items.
Completing the mini-game successfully triggers the `incrementInteraction` Server Action.
components/GlobalScore.tsx is implemented as a Server Component to display the global interaction stats fetched from the database.

title: Page Assembly and Refinement
summary: Assemble all components onto the main page, ensure responsive design, and perform final polish for animations and Vercel deployment readiness.
acceptanceCriteria: app/page.tsx integrates HeroReksio, MiniGame, and GlobalScore into a cohesive layout.
The layout is fully responsive and optimized for both mobile touchscreens and desktop mouse interactions.
The application builds successfully without errors (`next build`).
No hardcoded database connection strings, deployment paths, or secrets are present in the source code.
